user_query:
I’m the morning barista and I’m organizing supplies for our upcoming debate watch night. I dropped last week’s order log and an inventory snapshot into the workspace. Please write and run a small command-line script to automatically inspect the orders against inventory, flag anything we oversold or need to reorder, and summarize how our politically named drinks are performing.

Inputs:
- input/orders_week.csv (columns: order_id,timestamp,item,size,quantity)
- input/inventory_snapshot.csv (columns: item,starting_stock,reorder_point)

Requirements:
1) Implement a script (your choice of language) that can be run from the shell and placed at scripts/inspect_orders (use an appropriate extension like .py or .sh). It must:
   - Read both input files.
   - Classify an order as “political” if the item name contains any of these tokens: Filibuster, Bipartisan, Electoral, Gerrymander.
   - Aggregate total_sold per item across the entire input period.
   - Compute remaining_stock = starting_stock - total_sold.
   - Assign status per item:
       • "oversold" if remaining_stock < 0
       • "needs_reorder" if remaining_stock >= 0 and remaining_stock <= reorder_point
       • "ok" otherwise
   - Produce a machine-readable CSV of flagged items (status != "ok").
   - Compute daily counts of political vs non-political orders by date (YYYY-MM-DD extracted from the timestamp field) and identify the single top-selling political drink by total_sold.

2) Outputs to create:
   - outputs/inspection/reorder.csv with columns exactly: item,starting_stock,total_sold,remaining_stock,reorder_point,status. Include only items where status != "ok". Sort by status (alphabetically) then item.
   - outputs/inspection/report.md with:
       • A brief “Inventory Inspection Summary” that lists each item and its starting_stock, total_sold, remaining_stock, reorder_point, and status (include all items here, not just flagged ones).
       • A “Political Drinks Summary” that lists per-day totals of political vs non-political orders (YYYY-MM-DD with counts) and clearly names the top-selling political drink.
   - outputs/communications/staff_email.txt: a draft message to my coworkers that includes a Subject line and a short body. It must mention: (a) how many items were flagged in outputs/inspection/reorder.csv, (b) the list of flagged items with their statuses, and (c) the top-selling political drink. Close with a brief plan for the upcoming debate night.
   - outputs/run_command.txt: the exact command you used to run the script (e.g., "python3 scripts/inspect_orders.py input/orders_week.csv input/inventory_snapshot.csv ...").

Assumptions/clarifications:
- Do not use the internet; everything should run locally.
- Keep file paths and column names as specified above so I can re-run and verify.

Deliverables are the script at scripts/inspect_orders.*, the generated CSV and report under outputs/inspection/, the drafted email under outputs/communications/, and the run command log under outputs/. Thanks! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
